description:
A senior data engineer has asked you to take a point-in-time “pre-ETL” backup of the current incoming raw data folder.

    Current folder to back up  
      •  /home/user/etl_workspace/incoming/  
        ├── january.csv  
        ├── february.csv  
        ├── readme.txt  
        └── extras/  
            └── notes.csv  

    Desired backup artefact  
      •  A single gzip-compressed TAR archive named  
         /home/user/etl_workspace/backups/incoming_backup.tar.gz  

    Very important constraints  
      1. Perform the entire backup with ONE single shell command.  
      2. The resulting archive must preserve the full directory structure shown above.  
      3. The automated test will unpack the archive and compare its listing with the original
         structure.  The exact list expected is, in this order, produced by  
         “tar -tzf /home/user/etl_workspace/backups/incoming_backup.tar.gz”:  

         incoming/  
         incoming/january.csv  
         incoming/february.csv  
         incoming/readme.txt  
         incoming/extras/  
         incoming/extras/notes.csv  

    When you are finished, the only new artefact on disk should be the file  
    /home/user/etl_workspace/backups/incoming_backup.tar.gz.

    (Tip: you might need to create the “backups” directory first if it is absent, but that
    should be handled within the same single command that performs the archive.)
truth:
Existing state prepared for the agent:

    1. Directory tree already present before the task starts:
       /home/user/etl_workspace/
         ├── incoming/
         │     ├── january.csv        (content: "month,visits\nJan,1023\n")
         │     ├── february.csv       (content: "month,visits\nFeb,1107\n")
         │     ├── readme.txt         (content: "Raw CSV dump received from upstream.")
         │     └── extras/
         │           └── notes.csv    (content: "note_id,text\n1,'late arrival'\n")
         └── backups/                 (directory **does NOT** exist yet)

    2. After task completion the tester will verify:
       •  /home/user/etl_workspace/backups/incoming_backup.tar.gz exists.
       •  It is a valid gzip archive.
       •  `tar -tzf` on that archive produces exactly the following newline-separated list:

         incoming/
         incoming/january.csv
         incoming/february.csv
         incoming/readme.txt
         incoming/extras/
         incoming/extras/notes.csv

       •  No extra files are present in /home/user/etl_workspace/backups/ apart from the demanded archive.
name: task_000000_c7c0de03
